description_long: This object consists of four stylized coconut palm trees at different stages of growth, each with a slender, curved brown trunk and a crown of pale green, elongated leaves. The trunks display a smooth, slightly twisted appearance with subtle color gradations, and the bases are rounded and taper upward. The leaves are arranged in a radial pattern at the top, with each tree showing a different number of fronds and trunk curvature. The smallest tree is bent and close to the ground, while the tallest stands upright with a more pronounced canopy. The overall look is simplified and cartoon-like, with no visible coconuts or detailed bark texture. The set appears as a group, suggesting a progression from sapling to mature palm.
description: A group of four stylized coconut palm trees with smooth, curved brown trunks and pale green, radial leaves, each at a different growth stage.
description_view_0: Four palm trees are shown in a row, highlighting their varying heights and trunk curvatures, with the smallest tree bent and the tallest upright.
description_view_1: The trees are viewed from the side, emphasizing the curvature of the trunks and the layered arrangement of the leaves.
description_view_2: The trees are aligned to show their canopies from the front, making the differences in leaf spread and trunk thickness more apparent.
description_view_3: The trees are seen from an angle, accentuating the staggered heights and the arching form of the smallest tree.
attribute_husk_pattern: The trunks have a smooth, slightly twisted surface with subtle color gradations but lack visible fiber patterns or detailed markings. The surface appears uniform with no distinct husk texture.
attribute_growth_cluster: cluster of four, arranged in a line with varying heights and trunk curvatures
attribute_proximity_to_palm_trees: The coconuts are depicted as part of palm trees, with each coconut represented as the crown of a palm, directly attached to the trunk.
